title: The National Highways Act, 1956
full_text: The National Highways Act, 1956 राष्‍ट्रीय राजमार्ग अधिनियम, 1956 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 195648 Act Number: 48 Enactment Date: 1956-09-11 Act Year: 1956 Short Title: The National Highways Act, 1956 Hindi Title: राष्‍ट्रीय राजमार्ग अधिनियम, 1956 Long Title: An Act to provide for the declaration of certain highways to be national highways and for matters connected therewith. Ministry: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways Enforcement Date: 15-04-1957 Notification: 15th April, 1957, vide S.R.O. 1180(E), dated 4th April, 1957, see Gazette of India, 1957, Part II, s. 3(ii). Last Updated: 22-04-2019 Schedule 1. THE SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles The National Highways Act, 1956 Show all section Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Section 2. Declaration of certain highways to be national highways. Section 3. Definitions. Show Related Subordinates Section 3A. Power to acquire land, etc. Section 3B. Power to enter for survey, etc. Section 3C. Hearing of objections. Section 3D. Declaration of acquisition. Section 3E. Power to take possession. Section 3F. Right to enter into the land where land has vested in the Central Government. Section 3G. Determination of amount payable as compensation. Show Related Subordinates Section 3H. Deposit and payment of amount. Show Related Subordinates Section 3-I. Competent authority to have certain powers of civil court. Section 3J. Land Acquisition Act 1 of 1894 not to apply Section 4. National highways to vest in the Union Section 5. Responsibility for development and maintenance of national highways. Show Related Subordinates Section 6. Power to issue directions Section 7. Fees for services or benefits rendered on national highways. Show Related Subordinates Section 8. Omitted.. Section 8A. Power of Central Government to enter into agreements for development and maintenance of national highways. Show Related Subordinates Section 8B. Punishment for mischief by injury to national highway. Section 9. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 10. Laying of notifications, rules, etc., before Parliament. Schedule 1. THE SCHEDULE.
